Residential Window Cleaning in Sicamous: What You're Actually Dealing With

Sicamous sits at the junction of Shuswap and Mara Lakes, and that dual-lake position leaves a clear signature on residential glass. Mineral-laden spray travels inland from both shorelines, depositing calcium and silica on exterior panes that standard wiping cannot remove. Homes in the marina district and along Swansea Point face the heaviest exposure — properties here often need full interior and exterior service twice during the summer months rather than once. Vacation rental properties require particular attention before the peak tourism window opens in late May, when owners expect turnover-ready condition after a closed-off winter. Many Sicamous residences are multi-story builds with upper-level decks facing the water — proper extension equipment is essential to reach those elevated panes safely without damaging decking or railings below.

What is Residential Window Cleaning and Why Does It Matter in Sicamous?

Residential Window Cleaning in Sicamous involves removing the specific buildup that accumulates on glass in this area — mineral deposits from lake spray, pollen from surrounding forests, and the seasonal grime unique to the Shuswap's four-season climate. For Sicamous homeowners, clean windows aren't just cosmetic — they protect the glass surface from long-term etching and staining that becomes harder to remove over time.

Located at the junction of Shuswap and Mara Lakes, approximately 45 km east of Salmon Arm on Highway 1. Known as the 'Houseboat Capital of Canada.' This geography shapes how windows age here. Sicamous sits between two lakes — windborne moisture and mineral spray are significant. Higher tourism traffic in summer means commercial cleaning demand peaks sharply in June–August.

Between two lakes — both residential and commercial properties face heavy lake-spray mineral buildup. The calcium and silica left behind after lake water evaporates bonds to glass over time — standard cleaning won't remove it without the right treatment. Early intervention is always cheaper than remediation.

Professional residential addresses these challenges systematically. Where DIY cleaning smears mineral deposits or misses the frame and sill buildup, a proper clean removes every layer — leaving glass that's genuinely clear rather than just less dirty.

For Sicamous's mix of residential homes, vacation rentals, waterfront properties, and commercial tourism businesses (marinas, hotels, restaurants)., the investment in professional cleaning pays off both in appearance and in the long-term health of the glass itself.

What's Included in Our Residential Window Cleaning in Sicamous

Our residential in Sicamous is a complete clean from frame to sill — not just a wipe of the glass. Every job includes our standard process adapted to the specific conditions of your property, with a streak-free guarantee on every pane.

  • Exterior window cleaning — all accessible windows
  • Interior window cleaning — handled with care for your furnishings
  • Screen removal, cleaning, and reinstallation
  • Window track cleaning and wipe-down
  • Sill cleaning included at no extra charge
  • Streak-free squeegee finish on every pane

For lakefront and lake-view properties in Sicamous, we pay particular attention to panes facing the water — these accumulate mineral deposits faster than interior-facing windows and require more careful treatment to achieve a streak-free result.

We work around your schedule and access requirements. For properties with multiple stories or restricted access points, we discuss the approach before starting and will tell you upfront if a ladder or extension pole is needed.

Residential Window Cleaning Pricing in Sicamous

Window cleaning in Sicamous starts from $169 for exterior-only on a small home (up to 15 windows) and from $229 for full interior + exterior. We publish our prices upfront — no mystery quotes.

Mix of residential homes, vacation rentals, waterfront properties, and commercial tourism businesses (marinas, hotels, restaurants). Most Sicamous properties fall in the medium or large range — expect a full clean to run $199–$349 for a typical home in the area.

How We Do Residential Window Cleaning in Sicamous: Step by Step

Every Wagon Windows job follows the same process. Here's exactly what happens from the moment we arrive at your Sicamous property.

  1. 1. Walkthrough & Window Inventory

    Before touching anything, David walks the property with the homeowner to identify every window, flag any pre-existing damage, and confirm which areas are in-scope. This prevents disputes and ensures nothing is missed — especially on properties with skylights, hard-to-reach dormers, or custom glass panels common in Shuswap lakefront homes.

  2. 2. Screen Removal & Labelling

    Every screen is carefully removed and labelled by location — left-side bedroom, kitchen upper, etc. Labelling takes an extra minute per screen but eliminates the guesswork of reinstallation and prevents screens being forced into wrong frames, which damages splines. Screens are set aside in a safe, flat area during the clean.

  3. 3. Dry Debris Removal

    Before any water or solution is applied, loose debris — cottonwood fluff, dried insects, leaf fragments — is brushed away from frames, sills, and glass surfaces. Skipping this step turns debris into abrasive particles under the squeegee, which can scratch glass and leave streaks that require a second pass.

  4. 4. Pre-Rinse

    A light pre-rinse with clean water loosens mineral deposits, agricultural dust, and surface-level grime before the cleaning solution is applied. This is especially important on south-facing windows that accumulate UV-baked-on dust and pollen during Shuswap summers. Pre-rinsing reduces the work the solution has to do and extends its effectiveness.

  5. 5. Cleaning Solution Application

    A professional, eco-safe window cleaning solution is applied to the full pane using a wet applicator or sleeve mop. The solution lifts oils, mineral residue, and biological material that water alone cannot break down. Solution dwell time is calibrated to the level of buildup — heavier deposits get a longer soak before agitation.

  6. 6. Exterior Squeegee & Detail

    Each exterior pane is worked with a professional squeegee using overlapping, controlled strokes that pull solution and dissolved grime cleanly off the glass. Edges are hand-detailed with a lint-free cloth to catch any remaining moisture. Frames and sills are wiped down as part of this step — not as an afterthought.

  7. 7. Interior Window Cleaning

    Interior windows are cleaned with care for the home's furnishings — drop cloths or towels are placed on sills to catch drips, and solution is applied with a dampened applicator rather than a free-flowing mop to prevent overspray on walls or floors. Each pane is squeegeed and hand-detailed on the edges, with particular attention to upper corners where solution tends to pool.

  8. 8. Screen Reinstallation & Final Inspection

    Cleaned and dried screens are reinstalled by location using the labelling system from Step 2. David does a final walk around the property to inspect each window from the outside for any missed streaks or smears, correcting them on the spot. The homeowner is invited to do their own inspection before the job is signed off.

What sets us apart: Wagon Windows treats residential cleans as a complete service — screens labelled and reinstalled correctly, tracks wiped, and every window inspected before leaving — not a rushed rinse-and-go. David Hanna owns and runs the company himself, meaning the person on your property is accountable by name.

Common Residential Problems We Solve in Sicamous

  • Cottonwood pollen season coats every exterior window in a thick film that basic garden hose rinses cannot clear
  • Lake spray from Shuswap Lake deposits mineral-rich water on lakefront home windows that dries into white haze
  • Agricultural dust from nearby orchards and farms in the Salmon Arm corridor settles on glass and bakes on in summer heat
  • Wildfire smoke season leaves a greasy, oily film on glass that standard cleaning products struggle to break down
  • Interior condensation from high-humidity Shuswap summers causes mould and mineral buildup along window sills and lower frames

After a full residential clean, every window on the home is streak-free and clear — you notice the difference immediately when light comes through without the haze or smearing that built up over months. Screens sit flush in their frames, tracks are wiped clean, and the overall impression of the home from both inside and out is noticeably sharper. Most customers describe it as the house looking 'new again.'

When to Book Residential Window Cleaning in Sicamous

Most Sicamous properties benefit from residential 2–3 times per year: spring after pollen peaks (April–May), midsummer for dust and UV haze (July), and fall before freeze (September–October). Lakefront properties may need an additional clean in late spring or early summer due to higher mineral spray exposure from the water.

Sicamous sits between two lakes — windborne moisture and mineral spray are significant. Higher tourism traffic in summer means commercial cleaning demand peaks sharply in June–August.

Booking lead time for Sicamous: Because Sicamous is approximately 45 minutes from our Salmon Arm base, we schedule Sicamous jobs in batches when possible. We recommend booking at least 1–2 weeks in advance, especially during spring and fall peak periods.

To lock in your preferred date, book online via Calendly or call us at (403) 837-5092. We'll send a confirmation within a few hours.

Frequently Asked Questions — Residential in Sicamous

My Sicamous vacation rental needs a clean between every guest — is that something Wagon Windows can schedule?

We do offer recurring residential window cleaning for Sicamous vacation rental operators, though our scheduling depends on route timing. For between-guest cleans, we'd set up a recurring appointment tied to your turnover dates — typically exterior-only, 60–90 minutes, priced per visit. This works best with 48+ hours notice before each arrival. Summer is tight for scheduling in Sicamous — establish the recurring arrangement before June to guarantee availability.

How does living between Shuswap and Mara Lakes affect window maintenance for Sicamous homeowners?

Being flanked by two lakes doubles the mineral spray exposure compared to a single-lake community. Homes that face either lake — or even sit between them — collect mineral deposits from both directions depending on wind. We treat virtually all Sicamous exterior windows with a mineral pre-rinse as standard practice rather than assessing case-by-case. Factor in the higher tourism dust from the marina district and most Sicamous homes genuinely benefit from 3 cleans per year.

Do you clean residential windows in Swansea Point near Sicamous?

Yes — Swansea Point is on our Sicamous route. Properties there tend to be larger lakefront homes with significant mineral buildup from Mara Lake. We visit the Swansea Point area on scheduled Sicamous service days. Because the drive from Salmon Arm is approximately 45 minutes, we combine multiple jobs in the area for efficient scheduling — contact us for availability and we'll coordinate your booking with nearby visits.

What time of year should Sicamous homeowners book residential window cleaning?

May is the most important booking window for Sicamous homeowners — before summer tourism peaks and before lake spray from both Shuswap and Mara Lakes intensifies with warmer water. We see a sharp spike in Sicamous booking requests in June when it's already too late for ideal pre-season timing. A second clean in September catches the summer's mineral accumulation before freeze. Book your May appointment in March or April to guarantee your preferred date.

Do you clean both inside and outside windows for residential homes?

Yes — we offer exterior-only or full interior and exterior cleaning. Exterior-only is from $149 for small homes. Interior + exterior is from $199. Most customers choose full service for the best results.

Do I need to be home during the window cleaning?

For exterior-only cleaning, you don't need to be home as long as we have access to the outside of your property. For interior cleaning, someone should be present. Let us know your preference when booking.

Do you clean window screens and tracks for residential properties?

Screens and tracks are available as add-ons. Screens are $3–$4 each. Track cleaning starts at $50. These are often done at the same time as a window clean for the best results.

How often should I have my home's windows professionally cleaned?

Most Shuswap homeowners benefit from 2–3 cleans per year: spring (post-pollen), midsummer, and fall (pre-freeze). Lakefront homes may need an extra clean due to mineral spray.

Does Wagon Windows serve Sicamous?

Yes — Sicamous is within our service area, approximately 45 minutes from Salmon Arm. We serve residential homes, vacation rentals, and commercial businesses in Sicamous. Summer is peak season — book early.

Do you clean commercial windows for Sicamous businesses during tourist season?

Yes — we work with Sicamous businesses including storefronts, restaurants, and accommodation operators. Summer pre-season cleans (May) are high demand — book your commercial clean by April to guarantee availability.

Do Sicamous vacation rental properties need gutter cleaning before tourist season?

Yes — pre-season gutter cleaning in April or May is worth doing on Sicamous vacation rentals. Pine debris from the surrounding forests and winter storm material pack into gutters over the off-season. Blocked gutters that overflow during summer storms create fascia staining visible to guests and can damage the structure of rental-income properties.

What areas does Wagon Windows serve?

Wagon Windows serves the entire Shuswap region from our Salmon Arm base: Salmon Arm, Sorrento, Tappen, Blind Bay, Sicamous, Chase, Enderby, and Armstrong. Contact us if your area isn't listed — we travel for the right job.

How do I get a quote from Wagon Windows?

The fastest way is to book a free 30-minute consultation via our Calendly link at wagonwindows.com — you'll have a confirmed appointment within minutes. You can also call or text (403) 837-5092, or email davidhanna@wagonwindow.com. We respond within a few hours.

Is Wagon Windows insured?

Yes — Wagon Windows carries liability insurance. We treat your property with care and take full responsibility for our work on every job.
